comments.text: I posted a computational note on greedy $B_2[2]$ sets here: https://zenodo.org/records/18452185. Let $A\subset\mathbb{N}$ be constructed by the greedy rule: at step $k+1$, choose the smallest $m>a_k$ such that every sum $n$ has at most two representations $n=a+b$ with $a\le b$ and $a,b\in A\cup\{m\}$. The computation reaches $a_{2000}=7{,}445{,}662$, and the normalized count $|A\cap[1,N]|/\sqrt{N}$ stays around $0.7$ in this range (e.g. $\approx 0.733$ at $N=a_{2000}$). This supports the possibility that $\liminf_{N\to\infty}|A\cap[1,N]|/\sqrt{N}>0$, i.e. a counterexample to Problem 158 may exist.
